content:
{
  "title": "Fact-Chain Prompting: Stepwise Factuality Enhancement via Explicit Fact Anchoring",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) frequently generate hallucinated or unsupported content when prompted for factual information, especially on ambiguous or uncommon queries. Existing prompting techniques often fail to guide LLMs toward explicitly referencing verifiable facts.",
  "motivation": "Hallucination undermines trust and utility in LLMs for reliable information retrieval and critical decision-making tasks. As LLMs become widely adopted, robust methods to induce factual and attribution-aware outputs are urgently needed, yet prompt engineering for this remains underexplored.",
  "method": "We propose 'Fact-Chain Prompting', a method in which the prompt first instructs the LLM to enumerate a stepwise chain of atomic, verifiable facts relevant to the query, explicitly citing sources if available. Only after this enumeration does the LLM synthesize its answer, referencing the earlier fact chain. This two-part prompt separates fact recall from synthesis, encourages explicit attribution, and constrains generation to grounded information. Prompt templates and chain length will be ablated for effectiveness.",
  "experiment_plan": "Benchmarks will use open-domain factual QA datasets (e.g., TriviaQA, NaturalQuestions) and closed-book settings. Baselines include standard zero/few-shot, CoT, and retrieval-augmented prompting. Evaluation will measure factual accuracy, hallucination rate, and attribution coverage via established metrics and human raters."
}
